history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a safe floor cell, while the goal is farther ahead to the right. Moving forward keeps me in open space and brings me closer to the goal without risking lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward at the bottom-center of the view. The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. Since the goal is visible to the upper right, I should rotate to avoid the lava and orient toward the open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up with a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is blocked. The safest useful move is to turn right so I can start heading toward the open path and the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. The only safe open space around me is to the left side, but I need to turn before I can go there. I will turn left to face the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is a safe floor, while the goal is not directly reachable without moving first. Since moving forward advances me toward the visible open area and avoids the nearby lava, I will go ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
